## 1. Vision & Problem Statement

### The Problem

Every SaaS product shares the same foundational infrastructure needs: user authentication, session management, role-based access control, billing, and team/workspace management. As a developer building multiple products under the "Yo" brand, the cost of rebuilding this infrastructure for each new product is enormous — not just in initial development time, but in the long-term burden of maintaining security patches, compliance changes, billing logic, and admin tooling across multiple disconnected codebases.

Current reality without YoCore:
- YoSuite and YoPM each maintain their own auth logic, user tables, and billing integrations
- A user who signs up for both products has two separate identities with no unified experience
- Changes to billing logic (e.g., adding a new plan tier) must be made in every product independently
- There is no cross-product view: no single place to see total revenue, total users, or product health
- Building a third product (YoX) means rebuilding the same infrastructure a third time

### The Solution

YoCore is a centralized SaaS infrastructure platform — a private backend backbone — that abstracts authentication, authorization, billing, and organization management into a single, reusable system that any "Yo" product can connect to via a secure API.

Think of it as a private Auth0 + Stripe + Vercel Teams — purpose-built for the Yo ecosystem.

### Why This Matters

- **Speed:** New products go from idea to production-ready auth + billing in hours, not weeks
- **Consistency:** Every product shares the same user accounts, session logic, and billing primitives
- **Control:** As the sole Super Admin, you have a god-mode dashboard across all products
- **User Experience:** End users share a single verified email identity — one email works across all Yo products, with each product maintaining its own independent profile and credentials
- **Maintainability:** Security patches, compliance updates, and billing changes happen in one place

---

## 2. Goals & Non-Goals

### Goals (Phase 1)

- Provide centralized authentication (signup, signin, logout, password reset, email verification) for all Yo products
- Maintain a single verified email as the global identity anchor; all profile and credential data is per-product (no SSO — each product has its own independent login)
- Provide a role and permission system scoped per product
- Support workspace/organization management within each product
- Support billing plan management per product via Stripe, with upgrade/downgrade/cancellation flows
- Provide a Super Admin dashboard to manage all products, users, and billing from one place
- Expose a clean REST API (and optionally SDK) so new products can integrate in minimal time
- Support a Product Registry where new Yo products can be registered and issued API credentials
- Support user invitations to workspaces

### Goals (Future Phases)

> ⚠️ **Note (updated v1.10):** Items below were originally classified as future-phase. Several have since been promoted. Current status:
> - **Bundle packages — P0 (promoted v1.9, see §4.6 + Decision 20):** Super Admin bundle authoring + consumer checkout is now required for v1 launch. This line is kept for historical context.
> - **Product Admin panel — P1 (promoted v1.5, see §12):** Product-scoped admin panel. Data model shipped in Phase 1; UI is Phase 2.
> - **OAuth (Google/GitHub), Magic link — still Future Phase (Phase 3).**
> - **Analytics dashboard, cross-product search, announcements, white-label — still Future Phase.**

- OAuth login via Google and GitHub
- Magic link login
- ~~Bundle packages (cross-product subscriptions at a discount)~~ — **Promoted to P0 in v1.9 — see §4.6 + Decision 20**
- Product Admin panel (per-product admin access scoped to non-billing concerns) — **Promoted to P1 in v1.5**
- Analytics dashboard (signups, churn, revenue) per product and aggregated
- Cross-product user search and communication tools
- Global announcements and email broadcasts
- Potential white-label or public release of YoCore as a product

### Non-Goals (Phase 1)

- YoCore is NOT a public-facing product — end users interact with it only indirectly through Yo products
- YoCore will NOT provide a UI for end users (no "YoCore profile page" in Phase 1)
- YoCore will NOT handle product-specific business logic — only infrastructure concerns
- YoCore will NOT replace any product's own database for domain data (it stores only auth/billing/user primitives)
- YoCore will NOT support non-Yo third-party products connecting to it in Phase 1
- YoCore will NOT include a mobile SDK in Phase 1
- YoCore will NOT perform A/B testing, feature flagging, or analytics event tracking (those belong in each product)

## 3. User Personas

### Persona 1: Super Admin (The Developer/Owner — You)

**Name:** The Builder  
**Role:** Creator and sole owner of the Yo ecosystem  
**Access Level:** Unrestricted — full god-mode across all products  

**Context:**  
You are a solo developer or small team lead building multiple SaaS products under the Yo brand. You wear many hats: developer, product manager, and business operator. You need to move fast but also need visibility into what's happening across your entire portfolio.

**Goals:**
- Register new products quickly without duplicating infrastructure work
- Monitor the health and growth of all products from a single command center
- Manage billing plans centrally without touching each product's codebase
- Respond to user issues (e.g., account problems, billing disputes) from one place
- Create bundle deals across products to increase cross-sell revenue
- Broadcast announcements or operational messages to users across products

**Pain Points Without YoCore:**
- Switching between multiple admin dashboards to get a holistic view
- Implementing the same auth logic in every new product
- Billing configuration scattered across different Stripe accounts or product code
- No easy way to find a user across all products

**Technical Comfort:** High — comfortable with API keys, webhooks, developer tooling  

---

### Persona 2: Product Admin (Assigned Per Product)

**Name:** The Operator  
**Role:** Manager of a specific Yo product — could be a team member, a customer success person, or a business partner  
**Access Level:** Scoped to one product only  

**Context:**  
As YoCore grows and products scale, the Super Admin may delegate day-to-day product operations to a trusted person. This person needs to manage users and workspaces for their assigned product but must never have visibility into other products, billing infrastructure, or the overall platform.

> **Decision Note:** In Phase 1, the Product Admin role is defined in the data model but the product-specific admin panel is a Phase 2 deliverable. The Super Admin handles all operations initially.

**Goals:**
- View and manage users of their specific product
- Invite new users to workspaces
- Manage workspace settings and membership
- View basic usage stats for their product

**Pain Points:**
- Being blocked by the Super Admin for routine user management tasks
- No visibility into who is using the product and how

**Technical Comfort:** Medium — comfortable with web dashboards but not with APIs or code  

---

### Persona 3: End User (Customer)

**Name:** The Customer  
**Role:** User of one or more Yo products  
**Access Level:** Self-service only — their own account, sessions, subscriptions  

**Context:**  
An end user signed up for YoPM to manage their projects. They may later also subscribe to YoSuite. They expect a seamless, modern SaaS experience: fast signup, reliable login, easy billing management, and the ability to belong to multiple teams/workspaces.

**Goals:**
- Sign up and get started quickly (minimal friction)
- Log in once and access all Yo products they're subscribed to
- Manage their own subscription (upgrade, downgrade, cancel)
- Collaborate with teammates inside workspaces
- Know their account is secure (session visibility, logout all devices)

**Pain Points:**
- Creating a new account for every product they join
- Not knowing which devices are logged in
- Confusing or broken billing experiences
- Losing workspace access unexpectedly

**Technical Comfort:** Low to Medium — expects consumer-grade UX, not developer tooling

## 4. Core Features (Prioritized)

### Priority System
- **P0** — Must have for MVP. Without this, the product cannot function.
- **P1** — Should have shortly after MVP. Important for production readiness.
- **P2** — Nice to have / future roadmap.

---

### 4.1 Product Registry

| Priority | Feature |
|----------|---------|
| P0 | Register a new product (name, slug, logo URL, domain, description) |
| P0 | Generate API Key + Secret per product |
| P0 | API Key rotation and revocation |
| P0 | Product status management (Active, Inactive, Maintenance) |
| P1 | Webhook configuration per product (YoCore notifies product on events) |
| P1 | Product-specific settings (allowed redirect URIs, CORS origins) |
| P2 | Sandbox vs. Production environment per product (like Stripe test mode) |

### 4.2 Authentication

| Priority | Feature |
|----------|---------|
| P0 | User signup with email + password |
| P0 | Email verification (token-based, time-limited) |
| P0 | User signin with email + password |
| P0 | Secure password hashing — **Argon2id** (m=19456, t=2, p=1); run in worker thread pool, never block event loop |
| P0 | Constant-time response on signup/forgot-password regardless of email existence (prevents email enumeration via timing) |
| P0 | JWT-based session issuance with refresh token rotation |
| P0 | Logout (single device) |
| P0 | Forgot password flow (email reset link) |
| P0 | Password reset with time-limited token |
| P0 | Refresh token cookie: `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Domain=.yo.com` |
| P1 | Remember me (extended session duration) |
| P1 | Logout all devices |
| P1 | Active session list visible to user |
| P0 | Rate limiting on auth endpoints (brute-force protection) |
| P0 | Account lockout after N failed attempts |
| P0 | **Multi-factor authentication (TOTP) — required for SUPER_ADMIN, optional for END_USER** (Decision 16) |
| P0 | **MFA recovery codes** (10 single-use codes generated at enrollment, hashed at rest) |
| P2 | OAuth login — Google |
| P2 | OAuth login — GitHub |
| P2 | Magic link login (passwordless) |
| P2 | Passkey / WebAuthn support |

### 4.6 Billing & Subscription Management

| Priority | Feature |
|----------|---------|
| P0 | Create billing plans per product (name, price, billing cycle, feature limits) |
| P0 | Stripe integration (Customer, Subscription, PaymentIntent) |
| P0 | SSLCommerz integration for BDT payments — uses Stripe as billing calendar; renewal requires user-initiated payment via emailed SSLCommerz checkout link (48h expiry) |
| P0 | Subscribe a user or workspace to a plan |
| P0 | Free plan support (no payment method required) |
| P0 | Subscription status tracking (Active, Past Due, Canceled, Trialing) |
| P0 | Webhook handling for Stripe events (payment success, failure, cancellation) |
| P0 | Webhook idempotency — all inbound gateway webhooks deduplicated via `webhookEventsProcessed` collection + unique index; prevents double-activation or double-charge |
| P0 | SSLCommerz + Stripe desync protection — `invoices.pay` retried 24h with idempotency key; critical alert + PAST_DUE fallback after 24h failure |
| P0 | Distributed cron locks — all billing lifecycle crons (grace tick, trial tick, hold warnings, Day 85 deletion, audit archival) protected by `cronLocks` collection to prevent double-run on multi-instance deployment |
| P1 | Upgrade/downgrade plan flows (proration handling) |
| P1 | Cancel subscription (immediate or end-of-period) |
| P1 | Invoice retrieval per user/workspace |
| P1 | Failed payment retry logic and grace period |
| P1 | Trial period support per plan |
| P1 | Seat-based pricing (per-seat billing) — SSLCommerz seat changes billed at next renewal, not mid-cycle |
| P1 | Usage-based billing primitives (metered events reported by product) — `usageRecords` collection §1.31; `POST /v1/usage/records` (product-keyed); aggregate at period end via cron |
| **P0** | **Feature entitlement check** — `GET /v1/entitlements/:workspaceId` returns `planLimitsSnapshot` (grandfathered) or live `billingPlans.limits`; Redis-cached 60s; **promoted P1 → P0** (critical for SDK integration; without this, products implement their own plan interpretation = drift risk) |
| **P1** | **Plan grandfathering** — `subscriptions.planVersion` + `planSnapshotAt` + `planLimitsSnapshot`; existing subscribers keep limits at subscribe time; entitlement endpoint resolves snapshot-first |
| **P1** | **Plan archival cascade** — when a plan is archived, active subscribers are notified (email), given 30d grace, then auto-downgraded to fallback plan; `POST /v1/admin/plans/:id/preview-archive` dry-run first; see Flow AO |
| **P1** | **Tax ID / B2B VAT collection** — `customerTaxProfiles` collection; EU VAT ID + BD TIN + US EIN; Stripe TaxId.create on save; reverse-charge applied at checkout; `GET/PUT /v1/billing/tax-profile`; required for EU B2B sales |
| **P1** | **Public pricing page API** — `GET /v1/products/:slug/plans/public` (unauthenticated); returns only PUBLISHED+public plans; for landing pages; rate-limited 100/min per IP, cached 5m |
| **P0** | **Bundle packages — Super Admin authoring (CRUD + lifecycle: draft/publish/archive)** — see §4.6.1 |
| **P0** | **Bundle pricing models** — fixed amount, percent-discount, per-component override; per-currency variants (USD via Stripe, BDT via SSLCommerz) |
| **P0** | **Bundle eligibility & conflict resolution** at checkout — `block` / `cancel_and_credit` / `replace_immediately` when user has overlapping standalone subscription |
| **P0** | **Bundle pre-publish validation** — gateway price sync, component plan ACTIVE check, currency consistency, savings ≥ 0, scope match |
| **P0** | **Bundle visibility controls** — `public` / `unlisted` / `private` (Super-Admin-granted only) |
| **P0** | **Bundle change history** — append-only `changeHistory[]`; audit trail per edit |
| **P0** | **Bundle subscriber list & lookup** — admin endpoint to view all active subscribers per bundle |
| **P0** | **Bundle cancel cascade** (already in v1.8 via Flow AK — confirmed) |
| **P1** | **Bundle component plan-swap** (mid-cycle) — Super Admin changes component plan tier; grandfather vs forced-migrate paths |
| **P1** | **Standalone ↔ Bundle migration** (user-initiated) — upgrade standalone to bundle (credit), downgrade bundle to standalone(s) |
| **P1** | **Per-component seat overrides + bundle-wide trial** |
| **P1** | **Coupon × bundle interaction** — `coupons.appliesTo.bundleIds[]` |
| **P1** | **Bundle metrics dashboard** — attach rate, bundle MRR, churn-by-bundle, component revenue split |
| **P1** | **Bundle outbound webhooks** — `bundle.subscription.activated/canceled`, `bundle.component.swapped`, `bundle.archived` |
| P2 | Coupon and promotional code support |
| P2 | Annual vs. monthly billing toggle with discount |
| ~~P2~~ P1 | Tax handling (Stripe Automatic Tax + SSLCommerz VAT) — see `products.taxConfig` (already v1.8 P0 for Stripe); Tax ID collection `customerTaxProfiles` is new P1 |
| P2 | Revenue dashboard with MRR/ARR per product |
| P2 | Dunning management (automated retry + email sequence for failed payments) |

## 5. Detailed User Flows

### Flow 1: Signup & Email Verification

**Actor:** End User  
**Entry Point:** Signup form on any Yo product (e.g., YoPM's `/signup` page)

```
1. User fills in: First Name, Last Name, Email, Password, (optionally) Workspace name
2. Product (YoPM) sends POST /v1/auth/signup to YoCore with:
   - { email, password, name, product_id }
3. YoCore:
   a. Validates input (email format, password strength: min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number)
   b. Checks for existing account with that email across all products
   c. If email exists: returns error "An account with this email already exists. Sign in instead."
   d. Creates user record (status: UNVERIFIED)
   e. Hashes password with Argon2id
   f. Generates a 6-digit OTP or signed verification token (expires in 24h)
   g. Sends verification email via configured email provider (e.g., Resend)
   h. Returns: { user_id, status: "verification_pending" }
4. Product redirects user to "Check your email" screen
5. User clicks verification link in email
6. Link contains signed token → calls GET /v1/auth/verify-email?token=XXX
7. YoCore validates token (signature, expiry, not already used)
8. Updates user status to ACTIVE
9. Issues access_token (JWT, 15min expiry) + refresh_token (opaque, 7 days, httpOnly cookie)
10. Redirects user to product onboarding or dashboard
```

**Edge Cases:**
- Expired verification link → "Resend verification email" option
- User tries to sign in before verifying → prompt to verify, offer resend
- User clicks verification link a second time → idempotent success (already verified)

---

### Flow 2: Signin & Session Management

**Actor:** End User  
**Entry Point:** Login form on any Yo product

```
1. User enters email + password
2. Product sends POST /v1/auth/signin to YoCore with:
   - { email, password, product_id, remember_me: bool }
3. YoCore:
   a. Looks up user by email
   b. If not found: returns generic error (never reveal "email not found" — prevents enumeration)
   c. Verifies password against stored Argon2id hash
   d. If wrong password: increments failed_attempts counter
      - After 5 failures within 15min → lock account for 15min, notify user by email
   e. If account is unverified → return specific error prompting verification
   f. If account is suspended/banned → return clear error message
   g. Creates a new Session record with:
      - user_agent, IP address, device_fingerprint
      - expires_at: 15min (access) / 7 days (refresh) / 30 days if remember_me
   h. Returns: { access_token (JWT), refresh_token, user object }
4. Product stores access_token (memory or localStorage) and refresh_token (httpOnly cookie)
5. Product includes access_token as Bearer token on all subsequent API calls

Session Refresh:
1. Product detects access_token near expiry (or receives 401)
2. Sends POST /v1/auth/refresh with refresh_token
3. YoCore validates refresh_token (not expired, not revoked)
4. Issues new access_token + rotates refresh_token (old one is invalidated)
5. Returns new tokens
```

**Device Tracking:**
- Each session stores: IP, user agent, approximate location (from IP), created_at, last_used_at
- Super Admin can view active sessions per user
- User can view and terminate their own sessions (Phase 1: via API; Phase 2: via profile UI)

---

### Flow 3: Forgot Password / Reset

**Actor:** End User  
**Entry Point:** "Forgot Password?" link on signin page

```
1. User enters their email address
2. Product sends POST /v1/auth/forgot-password with { email, product_id }
3. YoCore:
   a. Looks up user by email
   b. ALWAYS returns success response (prevents email enumeration)
   c. If user found: generates a signed reset token (expires in 1 hour, one-time use)
   d. Stores token hash in DB with user_id and expiry
   e. Sends password reset email with link: https://yopm.com/reset-password?token=XXX
4. User clicks link in email
5. Product extracts token, shows new password form
6. User submits: POST /v1/auth/reset-password with { token, new_password }
7. YoCore:
   a. Validates token (signature, expiry, not already used)
   b. Validates new password strength
   c. Hashes new password, updates user record
   d. Invalidates token (marks as used)
   e. Revokes ALL active sessions for this user (security: someone else may have triggered this)
   f. Sends "Your password was changed" confirmation email
   g. Returns success
8. Product redirects user to signin page
```

---

### Flow 5: Admin Workspace-এ User Invite করা

**Actor:** Product Admin or Workspace Owner  
**Entry Point:** Workspace settings → Members → Invite  
**Visual:** HTML Flow 15

```
1. Admin invitee-র email + role (ADMIN / MEMBER / VIEWER) দিয়ে submit করে
   POST /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/invitations
   { email, role }  ← invited_by JWT থেকে নেওয়া হয়

2. YoCore guard checks (order matters):
   a. Caller-এর role check: workspaceMembers-এ OWNER বা ADMIN? না হলে 403
   b. Seat limit check:
      - workspaceMembers.countDocuments({ workspaceId, status: ACTIVE })
      - billingPlans.limits.maxMembers এর সাথে তুলনা (-1 = unlimited)
      - Limit-এ হলে: 402 "Upgrade plan to invite more members"
   c. Duplicate check:
      - users collection-এ email lookup
      - Found? workspaceMembers-এ already ACTIVE? → 409 "Already a member"

3. Invitation record তৈরি:
   - crypto.randomBytes(32) → rawToken
   - sha256(rawToken) → tokenHash (DB-তে hash store, never plain token)
   - invitations collection: { tokenHash, workspaceId, email, role, invitedBy,
     expiresAt: now+72h, status: PENDING, isExistingUser }
   - Pending invitation আগে থাকলে replace করো (resend case)

4. Invitation email পাঠাও:
   - isExistingUser = true  → "Accept Invitation" link
     https://{product}/invitations/accept?token=rawToken
   - isExistingUser = false → "Accept & Create Account" link
     https://{product}/invitations/signup?token=rawToken

── PATH A: Existing YoCore User ──────────────────────────────
5a. User link click করে → POST /v1/invitations/accept { token }
    YoCore: sha256(token) → DB lookup → expiry check
    Expired → 410 "Invitation expired. Ask admin to resend."

6a. Token valid:
    workspaceMembers INSERT { userId, workspaceId, roleId, addedBy, joinedAt }
    productUsers UPSERT { userId, productId }  ← product link ensure
    invitations UPDATE { status: ACCEPTED, acceptedAt }
    auditLogs INSERT { action: "workspace.member.joined", method: "invitation" }
    → Redirect to workspace dashboard

── PATH B: New User ───────────────────────────────────────────
5b. User link click → GET /v1/invitations/preview?token=rawToken
    Response: { email (locked), workspaceName, productName, role }
    UI: email field locked, user fills name + password

6b. POST /v1/invitations/accept-new { token, name, password }
    users INSERT {
      name, email, passwordHash: argon2id(password),
      emailVerified: true,          ← invitation = implicit trust (Decision 4)
      emailVerifiedAt: now,
      emailVerifiedMethod: "invitation"
    }
    productUsers INSERT { userId, productId, status: ACTIVE }
    workspaceMembers INSERT { userId, workspaceId, roleId, addedBy }
    invitations UPDATE { status: ACCEPTED, acceptedAt }
    auditLogs INSERT { action: "user.created.via.invitation" }
    Auto-login: issue access token + refresh token
    → Redirect to onboarding

7. Admin-কে notification email: "[email] accepted your invitation"
8. Expired invitation → Admin dashboard-এ "Resend" option (নতুন token তৈরি হয়)
```

**DB Collections touched:** `invitations`, `users`, `workspaceMembers`, `productUsers`, `subscriptions`, `billingPlans`, `auditLogs`

**Security notes:**
- Token: raw token শুধু email-এ যায়, DB-তে hash store
- Seat limit check: race condition থেকে বাঁচতে DB-level atomic check
- Expired invitation: 410 status, admin-কে resend করতে বলো

---

### Flow 6: Subscribing to a Plan

**Actor:** End User  
**Entry Point:** Product's Pricing page or in-app upgrade prompt

```
1. User selects a plan (e.g., "Pro — $29/month")
2. Product calls POST /v1/billing/checkout to YoCore with:
   - { user_id, workspace_id (optional), plan_id, product_id }
3. YoCore:
   a. Creates or retrieves Stripe Customer for this user
   b. Creates a Stripe Checkout Session with:
      - success_url, cancel_url (back to product)
      - plan's price_id (mapped internally)
      - customer email pre-filled
   c. Returns: { checkout_url }
4. Product redirects user to Stripe Checkout (hosted page)
5. User enters card details on Stripe's UI (YoCore/Product never touches raw card data)
6. Stripe processes payment
7. On success: Stripe redirects to success_url
8. Stripe sends webhook to YoCore: `checkout.session.completed`
9. YoCore:
   a. Validates webhook signature (Stripe-Signature header)
   b. Retrieves subscription details
   c. Updates user/workspace subscription record in DB:
      - plan_id, stripe_subscription_id, stripe_customer_id, status: ACTIVE, current_period_end
   d. Sends "Subscription activated" email to user
   e. Fires internal webhook to product: `subscription.activated` with plan details
10. Product unlocks features based on new plan

Failed Payment & Suspension Lifecycle:

Payment Failure Grace Period (triggered by `invoice.payment_failed` webhook):
- YoCore sets subscription status → PAST_DUE
- Day 1:  Send "payment_failed_day_1" email — "We couldn't process your payment. Stripe will retry."
- Day 5:  Send "payment_failed_day_5" email — "Still failing. Update your payment method to avoid suspension."
- Day 7:  Send "payment_failed_day_7" email — "Final warning. Subscription will be canceled tomorrow."
- If payment succeeds at any point (`invoice.payment_succeeded`): clear all flags, status → ACTIVE, send confirmation email

Account Suspension (triggered by `customer.subscription.deleted` webhook):
- YoCore sets workspace.active = false, workspace.account_suspended = true, workspace.suspension_date = now
- Day 30: Send "account_suspended_day_30" email — remind of suspension, offer reactivation link
- Day 60: Send "account_suspended_day_60" email — "Your data will be permanently deleted in 25 days."
- Day 85: **PERMANENT DATA DELETION** — delete all workspace data, users, documents, roles; cancel any remaining Stripe records; set workspace.data_deleted = true; this cannot be undone

Note: workspace.trial_converted flag is set to true when a trial workspace successfully subscribes to a paid plan. This flag prevents trial expiry jobs from processing already-converted workspaces.
```

---

### Flow 7: Subscribing to a Bundle

**Actor:** End User  
**Entry Point:** Bundle pricing page (hosted on a marketing site or within a Yo product)

```
1. User views bundle details (e.g., "Yo Bundle: YoPM Pro + YoSuite Pro — $49/month")
2. User clicks "Subscribe to Bundle"
3. Product (or marketing site) calls POST /v1/billing/bundles/{bundle_id}/checkout with:
   - { user_id }
4. YoCore:
   a. Validates bundle is still active
   b. Creates Stripe Checkout Session for the bundle's combined price
   c. Returns checkout_url
5. Same Stripe Checkout flow as above
6. On success webhook (`checkout.session.completed`):
   a. YoCore creates a BundleSubscription record
   b. Creates individual product subscriptions for each product in the bundle
      (user gets access to all included products at included plan tiers)
   c. Sends unified "Bundle activated" email listing all included products
   d. Fires `subscription.activated` webhook to each product in the bundle
7. User can access all bundled products immediately

Billing:
- Bundle appears as a single line item on invoices
- YoCore internally tracks bundle vs. individual subscriptions
- Downgrading a bundle: user must cancel bundle and re-subscribe to individual products
```

---

### Flow 8: Super Admin Registering a New Product

**Actor:** Super Admin  
**Entry Point:** YoCore Dashboard → Products → "Register New Product"

```
1. Super Admin fills out registration form:
   - Product Name (e.g., "YoAnalytics")
   - Slug (e.g., "yoanalytics") — auto-generated, editable, must be unique
   - Domain (e.g., "yoanalytics.com")
   - Logo URL or upload
   - Description
   - Status: Active / Inactive (default: Inactive until configured)
2. Submits form
3. YoCore:
   a. Creates Product record in DB
   b. Generates API Key (prefix: yc_live_) and API Secret (shown ONCE, then hashed)
   c. Returns product dashboard with credentials displayed in a modal
4. Super Admin copies API Key + Secret (stores securely, e.g., in product's .env)
5. Product is now registered and appears on dashboard (in Inactive state)
6. Super Admin integrates YoCore SDK/API into the new product's codebase
7. Super Admin marks product as Active once integration is tested
8. Product appears on the "All Products" overview

Security Notes:
- API Secret is hashed server-side immediately after display (like Stripe's approach)
- Super Admin can generate a new secret at any time (old one is invalidated after confirmation)
- API Keys are scoped to their product — cannot be used to access other products' data
```

---

### Flow 9: Super Admin Creating a Billing Plan

**Actor:** Super Admin  
**Entry Point:** YoCore Dashboard → [Product] → Billing → Plans → "Create Plan"

```
1. Super Admin fills out plan creation form:
   - Plan Name (e.g., "Pro")
   - Display Price (e.g., $29.00)
   - Billing Cycle: Monthly / Annual / One-time
   - Currency (default: USD)
   - Trial Period (days): 0 = no trial
   - Is Free Plan: toggle (if true, price fields hidden)
   - Feature Limits:
     - Max Workspaces: number or "Unlimited"
     - Max Seats per Workspace: number or "Unlimited"
     - Max [custom resource]: number or "Unlimited" (product-defined)
   - Stripe Price ID (if already created in Stripe) OR
     "Create in Stripe Automatically" toggle
2. If auto-create in Stripe:
   a. YoCore calls Stripe API to create Product + Price
   b. Stores returned price_id against the plan
3. Plan is saved in DB with status: DRAFT
4. Super Admin previews plan, then publishes it (status: ACTIVE)
5. Plan is now available for users to subscribe to within that product

Plan Editing Rules:
- Name and limits can be edited anytime (only affects new subscribers)
- Price cannot be edited (Stripe constraint) — must archive old plan and create new one
- Archiving a plan: existing subscribers stay on old plan until they change; new subscriptions to this plan are blocked
```

---

### Flow 10: Super Admin Creating a Bundle (v1.9 expanded)

**Actor:** Super Admin
**Entry Point:** YoCore Dashboard → Overview → Bundles → “Create Bundle”
**Goal:** Compose 2–10 product+plan combinations into one priced offering with conflict resolution and currency variants.

```
0. Pre-flight (cached on screen load):
   - Load all ACTIVE products + their published plans
   - Group plans by product.billingScope (workspace vs user)
   - Bundle can only mix plans of the SAME billingScope (enforced)

1. Super Admin fills out bundle form:
   - Name (e.g., "Yo Power Bundle")
   - Slug (auto-generated, editable, unique)
   - Description / marketing copy / hero image URL
   - Visibility: public (listed) | unlisted (link-only) | private (invite-only)
   - Components (min 2, max 10):
     * YoSuite → Business Plan ($39/mo)
     * YoHM → Pro Plan ($19/mo)
     * YoCRM → Starter Plan ($15/mo)
     * Sum of standalone = $73/mo
   - Pricing model (choose one):
     a) FIXED: bundle price = $49/mo (savings auto-shown: 33% off $73)
     b) PERCENT_DISCOUNT: 30% off sum (auto = $51.10/mo)
     c) PER_COMPONENT_OVERRIDE: YoSuite $25 + YoHM $14 + YoCRM $10 = $49 (gives per-product revenue split for accounting)
   - Currency variants:
     * USD via Stripe (required)
     * BDT via SSLCommerz (optional, separate price: ৳3,999/mo for example)
     * Each variant gets its own gatewayPriceIds entry
   - Billing cycle: monthly | annual (annual auto-applies extra 15% if configured)
   - Trial: bundle-wide trialDays (default 0; if set, applies on first checkout)
   - Per-component seat overrides (P1):
     * YoSuite: 5 seats included (vs plan default 3)
     * YoHM: unified seat = uses bundle parent seat count
   - Eligibility policy (when user has overlapping standalone sub):
     * BLOCK (default) — show "You already subscribe to YoSuite. Cancel first."
     * CANCEL_AND_CREDIT — cancel old at period end, prorated unused credit applied to bundle
     * REPLACE_IMMEDIATELY — cancel old now, no credit, bundle starts fresh
   - Max redemptions (optional, total across all users)
   - Availability window: startsAt / endsAt (null = no limit)
   - Tags / metadata (free-form for marketing analytics)

2. Super Admin clicks "Validate & Preview"
3. YoCore runs PRE-PUBLISH VALIDATION:
   a. All component products status = ACTIVE
   b. All component plans status = PUBLISHED (not DRAFT/ARCHIVED)
   c. All components share same billingScope
   d. Sum-of-standalone > bundle price (savings >= 0); allow 0 with confirmation
   e. Each currency variant has all required gatewayPriceIds creatable on its gateway
   f. No two components for the same product (e.g., not YoSuite Pro + YoSuite Business)
   g. trialDays consistent (warn if any component plan has its own trial)
   → Returns 422 with field-level errors if any check fails

4. Preview screen shows:
   - Live pricing table per currency
   - Savings % badge
   - Component breakdown
   - Eligibility-impact estimate (e.g., "X existing standalone subscribers will be blocked / migrated")
   - JSON snippet for marketing site embed

5. Super Admin clicks "Save as Draft" OR "Publish Now"
   - DRAFT: stored, not visible publicly, no gateway prices created yet
   - PUBLISH:
     a. YoCore creates Stripe Product + per-currency Prices (one per currency variant)
     b. For BDT variant: registers with SSLCommerz (or marks as "manual link request" mode)
     c. Persists gatewayPriceIds on bundle record
     d. Status = ACTIVE
     e. Audit log: bundle.published
     f. Outbound webhook to all component products: bundle.created (with bundleId, components, eligibility)

6. Bundle now visible per visibility setting:
   - public: shown on /v1/billing/bundles listing (any product can render on its pricing page)
   - unlisted: only retrievable by direct ID/slug
   - private: requires Super Admin POST /v1/admin/bundles/:id/grant-access

7. Edit flow (PATCH /v1/admin/bundles/:id):
   - Cosmetic edits (name, description, hero) — free, no audit trail
   - Pricing change — confirms with "X active subscribers will see new price at next renewal" + audit + webhook bundle.price_updated
   - Component swap (P1) — see Flow AM
   - Visibility flip — audit-logged

8. Archive flow (POST /v1/admin/bundles/:id/archive):
   - Bundle removed from listings; existing subscribers continue (not auto-canceled)
   - New checkouts blocked
   - Audit + webhook bundle.archived
   - Hard delete only allowed if zero ever-active subscriptions (otherwise soft-archive forever)
```

**Key safety rules:**
- Once published, bundle slug & components order are immutable (audit-friendly).
- Pricing changes affect *renewals only*, never mid-cycle existing subscribers.
- Component swap uses Flow AM with explicit grandfather-vs-migrate decision.
- Currency variants can be added later via PATCH; can never be removed if active subs exist on that currency.

---

### Flow 11: Existing User Signs Up for a Second Product (Same Email)

**Actor:** End User who already has a YoCore account (e.g., YoPM user), signing up for a second Yo product (e.g., YoSuite)  
**Entry Point:** Signup form on a product the user hasn't joined yet

> This is a critical flow. Since all credentials are now per-product, we cannot ask the user to "prove ownership" by entering another product's password. Instead, YoCore sends a short-lived confirmation email to the already-verified address. See also Decision 13 for the full architectural explanation.

**The critical UX note: Email verification is global — once verified, it stays verified. No re-verification needed for a second product. However, the user must set a new product-specific password.**

```
1. User submits signup form on YoSuite:
   { email, name, password, product_id: yosuite }

2. YoSuite sends POST /v1/auth/signup to YoCore

3. YoCore:
   a. Finds existing users record with that email (emailVerified: true)
   b. Does NOT check or compare any password — credentials are per-product
   c. Checks productUsers: { userId, productId: yosuite }
      - Already exists → 409 "You already have an account in this product. Sign in instead."
      - Does not exist → proceed

   d. Hashes the submitted password (Argon2id). Stores it in a short-lived authTokens record:
      type: "product_join_confirm"
      payload: { pendingPasswordHash, pendingName, pendingProductId: "yosuite" }
      expiresAt: now + 1h

   e. Sends confirmation email to the EXISTING verified email address:
      Subject: "Confirm your YoSuite account"
      Body: "Someone used your email to sign up for YoSuite. Click below to confirm."
      (If this wasn't you — no action needed, the link expires in 1 hour)

   f. Returns: { status: "confirm_email_sent" }

4. User clicks the confirmation link → POST /v1/auth/confirm-product-join { token }

5. YoCore:
   a. Validates the token (not used, not expired)
   b. Creates productUsers record with the hashed password + name from the token payload
   c. Issues JWT + refresh token for YoSuite session
   d. Returns: { access_token, user, status: "second_product_joined" }

6. YoSuite shows workspace setup wizard
   POST /v1/auth/finalize-onboarding creates a YoSuite-scoped workspace
```

**MongoDB state BEFORE (user exists in YoPM only):**
```
users:        { _id: usr_karim, email: karim@example.com, emailVerified: true }  ← exists
productUsers: { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yopm, passwordHash: "...",
                name: { first: "Karim" }, status: ACTIVE }                        ← exists
sessions:     { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yopm, ... }                   ← exists
workspaces:   { _id: ws_yopm, productId: prod_yopm, ownerId: usr_karim }         ← exists
```

**MongoDB state AFTER (user confirmed and joined YoSuite):**
```
users:        { _id: usr_karim, email: karim@example.com, emailVerified: true }  ← UNCHANGED
productUsers: { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yopm,    passwordHash: "A" }  ← unchanged
              { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yosuite, passwordHash: "B" }  ← NEW (independent)
sessions:     { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yopm }                        ← unchanged
              { userId: usr_karim, productId: prod_yosuite }                     ← NEW SESSION
workspaces:   { productId: prod_yopm,    ownerId: usr_karim }                    ← unchanged
              { productId: prod_yosuite,  ownerId: usr_karim }                    ← NEW WORKSPACE
authTokens:   token used + marked usedAt (expires naturally)
```

**Email sent:**
- "Confirm your YoSuite signup" — confirmation link to the verified address (1h expiry, single-use)

**Edge Cases:**
- Confirmation link expired → user must restart signup
- productUsers already exists by the time the link is clicked (race condition) → 409, redirect to signin
- users.emailVerified = false (rare edge case) → run full email verification flow instead (same as first product signup)

---

### Flow 12: Onboarding Finalization

**Actor:** End User who just verified their email for the first time  
**Entry Point:** Automatic redirect after email verification

> Without this flow, users land on a half-configured experience — no workspace name, no timezone, nothing set up. YoCore's `finalize_onboarding` endpoint handles this explicitly.

```
1. User verifies email (tokens issued, redirected to product)
2. Product checks productUsers.onboarded === false (returned in user session data)
3. If not onboarded: product shows onboarding wizard (product-side UI)
   Typical steps:
   - Step 1: Confirm/set display name
   - Step 2: Set workspace name (or join existing workspace)
   - Step 3: Set timezone + date/time format preferences
   - Step 4: (Optional) Invite teammates
4. On completion, product calls POST /v1/auth/finalize-onboarding with:
   - { user_id, workspace_name, timezone, date_format, time_format }
5. YoCore:
   a. Updates productUsers.onboarded = true (per-product flag, not global)
   b. Creates default workspace if workspace_name provided (or skips if joining existing)
   c. Adds user as OWNER of the new workspace
   d. Updates user timezone/format preferences
   e. Sends "welcome" email with getting started tips
   f. Returns: { user (updated), workspace }
6. Product redirects to main dashboard

Important: Until onboarding is complete, YoCore allows limited API access:
- Auth middleware supports an `allowUnverified` flag for onboarding-stage endpoints
- Endpoints that require onboarding: all standard product features
- Endpoints allowed before onboarding complete: profile update, workspace setup, resend verification
```

**The `allowUnverified` Pattern:**
Products can call certain YoCore endpoints even when the user's email is unverified or onboarding is incomplete — specifically for the onboarding wizard itself. YoCore's middleware checks:
```
verify(role, workspace, 'unverified')  → allows unverified users through
verify(role, workspace)                → blocks unverified users (default)
```
This prevents the paradox where a user can't complete onboarding because they're blocked until they complete onboarding.

---

### Flow 13: Switch Workspace / Switch Product

**Actor:** End User who belongs to multiple workspaces or multiple Yo products  
**Entry Point:** Workspace/product switcher in any Yo product's UI

```
1. User clicks workspace switcher → sees list of their workspaces in the current product
   (or a product switcher showing all Yo products they have access to)
2. User selects a different workspace
3. Product calls POST /v1/auth/switch-workspace with:
   - { target_workspace_id, product_id }
   - Current access_token as Bearer
4. YoCore:
   a. Validates user is a member of target_workspace_id
   b. Validates workspace is ACTIVE
   c. Retrieves user's role in the target workspace
   d. Issues a new access_token scoped to target workspace:
      - { sub: user_id, workspace_id: target_workspace_id, role: new_role, plan: workspace_plan }
   e. Refresh token remains the same (not rotated on workspace switch)
   f. Returns: { access_token (new), workspace: { id, name, plan, role } }
5. Product updates local session context with new access_token
6. Product reloads with data scoped to the new workspace

Switching between Products:
1. Yo products are fully independent — each product has its own separate login session
2. A user navigating from YoPM to YoSuite must sign in to YoSuite separately
3. Same email and password may be set in each product, but the sessions are not shared
```

**Edge Cases:**
- User tries to switch to a suspended workspace → error: "This workspace is currently suspended"
- User is removed from workspace while session is active → next switch attempt returns 403, frontend clears session for that workspace

---

## 6. Super Admin Dashboard — Screen Breakdown

### Screen 1: Dashboard Home / All Products Overview

**Purpose:** Command center — see the state of the entire Yo ecosystem at a glance  
**URL:** `/dashboard`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Total active users (all products combined)
- Total MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue, all products)
- Total active subscriptions
- Product cards grid: for each product — name, logo, active users, MRR, status badge

**Actions Available:**
- Click a product card → go to Product Detail
- Register New Product (button)
- View All Users (cross-product search)
- View Bundles
- View Global Announcements
- Account settings (Super Admin profile, 2FA, API Keys)

---

### Screen 2: Product Detail Home

**Purpose:** Overview of one specific product  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{product_slug}`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Product name, logo, status, domain, slug
- Stats: active users, active subscriptions, MRR, new signups (last 30 days)
- Quick-access tiles: Users, Workspaces, Billing Plans, Settings

**Actions Available:**
- Edit product details (name, logo, domain)
- Change product status (Active / Inactive / Maintenance)
- Regenerate API credentials
- Delete product (confirmation required — destructive)

---

### Screen 3: Product → Users

**Purpose:** View and manage all users of a specific product  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/users`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Paginated user table: name, email, status (Active/Suspended/Banned), plan, workspaces, last login, joined date
- Search bar (by name or email)
- Filters: by status, by plan, by workspace

**Actions Available:**
- View user detail
- Suspend / Unsuspend user
- Ban user
- Reset user's password (triggers reset email)
- Force logout all sessions
- Impersonate user (Phase 2 — see Section 11)
- Remove user from product

---

### Screen 4: Product → User Detail

**Purpose:** Deep view into a single user's activity and settings within a product  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/users/{user_id}`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Profile: name, email, avatar, timezone, account status
- Workspaces they belong to (role in each)
- Current subscription plan, billing status, next renewal date
- Active sessions (IP, device, last used, location)
- Recent activity log (login events, plan changes, etc.)

**Actions Available:**
- Edit profile fields
- Change role in a workspace
- Add to a workspace
- Remove from a workspace
- Suspend / Ban account
- Terminate specific sessions
- View invoices
- Manually change subscription plan (override billing)

---

### Screen 5: Product → Workspaces

**Purpose:** View and manage all workspaces within a product  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/workspaces`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Paginated workspace table: name, slug, owner, member count, plan, created date, status
- Search and filter by plan, member count, status

**Actions Available:**
- View workspace detail
- Edit workspace name/settings
- Transfer ownership
- Suspend / Delete workspace
- Add/remove members

---

### Screen 6: Product → Workspace Detail

**Purpose:** Manage a specific workspace  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/workspaces/{workspace_id}`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Workspace name, slug, owner, created at
- Member list: name, email, role, joined date
- Current plan, seat count vs. limit, subscription status

**Actions Available:**
- Invite member
- Change member role
- Remove member
- Change workspace plan (manual override)
- Delete workspace

---

### Screen 7: Product → Billing Plans

**Purpose:** Manage pricing tiers for a product  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/billing`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Plan cards: name, price, billing cycle, active subscribers, status (Active/Archived/Draft)
- Summary: total revenue, subscriber distribution across plans

**Actions Available:**
- Create new plan
- Edit plan details (name, limits)
- Archive plan
- View subscribers on a plan

---

### Screen 8: Product → Billing → Plan Detail

**Purpose:** Inspect and edit a specific plan  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/billing/plans/{plan_id}`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Plan details: name, price, cycle, trial, feature limits
- Subscriber count, MRR from this plan
- Stripe Price ID (read-only reference)

**Actions Available:**
- Edit name / feature limits
- Archive plan
- View subscriber list

---

### Screen 9: Product → Settings

**Purpose:** Configure product-level settings and API credentials  
**URL:** `/dashboard/products/{slug}/settings`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- API Key (masked), creation date, last used date
- Webhook URL, secret
- Allowed redirect URIs
- CORS origins
- Email settings (from name, from address for this product's transactional emails)

**Actions Available:**
- Reveal / copy API Key
- Rotate API Secret
- Add/remove webhook URLs
- Add/remove redirect URIs
- Save settings

---

### Screen 10: Overview → All Users (Cross-Product Search)

**Purpose:** Find any user across all products  
**URL:** `/dashboard/users`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Search results table: name, email, products they're in, status, last login
- User profile summary on click

**Actions Available:**
- View full user detail (opens per-product user detail)
- Quick suspend/ban

---

### Screen 11: Overview → Bundles (v1.9 expanded)

**Purpose:** List + manage cross-product bundle packages (Super Admin authoring)
**URL:** `/dashboard/bundles`

**Key Data Displayed (table view):**
- Bundle list: name, slug, status (DRAFT/ACTIVE/ARCHIVED), components (icon row of products), pricing model (FIXED/%/OVERRIDE), price + currency variants count, savings %, active subscribers, MRR contribution, attach rate (last 30d), visibility badge
- Filters: status, visibility, product included, currency
- Sort: created date / MRR / subscribers / attach rate
- Search: by name or slug

**Top widgets:**
- Total bundle MRR (vs single-product MRR for comparison)
- Best-performing bundle (by attach rate)
- Bundle distribution chart (% of new signups choosing each bundle vs single)

**Actions Available:**
- **Create new bundle** → opens Flow 10 wizard
- **Edit bundle** → PATCH; pricing/component changes show "X subscribers affected" warning
- **Preview** → read-only validation + pricing simulation
- **Publish / Archive** → lifecycle transitions
- **Sync gateway prices** → re-create Stripe/SSLCommerz prices if drifted
- **View subscribers** → paginated list with cancel-cascade preview
- **View metrics** → attach rate, churn, component revenue split (Screen 11a)
- **Grant access** (private bundles) → user/workspace whitelist
- **Component swap** (P1) → opens Flow AM modal with grandfather/migrate choice

---

### Screen 11a: Bundle Detail (NEW v1.9)

**Purpose:** Deep-dive analytics + lifecycle management for one bundle
**URL:** `/dashboard/bundles/:id`

**Tabs:**
1. **Overview** — components, pricing per currency, savings %, eligibility policy, change history timeline
2. **Subscribers** — paginated active + canceled list; filter by status / currency / signup date; per-row "force cancel cascade" (with audit reason)
3. **Metrics** — attach rate trend, MRR per currency, churn cohort, component revenue split, conversion funnel (preview → checkout → activated)
4. **Edit** — form with diff view; high-impact changes flagged red
5. **Webhooks** — outbound delivery log filtered to this bundle's events
6. **Audit** — every change with actor, before/after snapshot, correlation ID

**Actions:** publish, archive, sync prices, swap component (P1), grant private access, export subscriber CSV, force-cascade-cancel single subscriber.

---

### Screen 12: Overview → Announcements

**Purpose:** Send announcements or emails to users across products  
**URL:** `/dashboard/announcements`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Past announcements list: subject, audience, sent date, delivery stats (opens, clicks)

**Actions Available:**
- Compose new announcement
  - Target: All Products / Specific Products / Specific Plans / All Users
  - Channel: In-app banner / Email / Both
- Schedule announcement
- Preview before send

---

### Screen 13: Super Admin → Personal Settings

**Purpose:** Manage Super Admin account security  
**URL:** `/dashboard/settings`

**Key Data Displayed:**
- Profile: name, email
- Security: active sessions, 2FA status
- Notification preferences

**Actions Available:**
- Enable/disable 2FA (TOTP via authenticator app — strongly recommended)
- Manage active sessions
- Change password
